rows.note: current served article-closeout stays accepted-outcome-ready and can issue validator-bound economic receipts under the existing exact-compute envelope; publication note: profile `tassadar.internal_compute.article_closeout.v1` is the current served internal-compute profile and remains compatible with the current exact-compute mount, accepted-outcome, and market envelope refs

deterministic import subset is now profile-specific and validator-attachable, but it stays candidate-only until the challenge window closes and the profile-specific authority envelope is satisfied; publication note: profile `tassadar.internal_compute.deterministic_import_subset.v1` stays suppressed as a served capability, but now has explicit profile-specific route, mount, and accepted-outcome templates for bounded public use under named policy and portability envelopes

profile remains suppressed pending explicit profile-specific mount, accepted-outcome, and economic policy artifacts; publication note: profile `tassadar.internal_compute.generalized_abi.v1` is benchmarked enough to name publicly, but broader mount policy and accepted-outcome templates must stay explicit before it can widen the current served lane

portable broad family stays refused because portability evidence and public authority posture are still incomplete; publication note: profile `tassadar.internal_compute.portable_broad_family.v1` remains refused for broader route publication because portability, evidence, or refusal posture is still incomplete

profile remains suppressed pending explicit profile-specific mount, accepted-outcome, and economic policy artifacts; publication note: profile `tassadar.internal_compute.public_broad_family.v1` is benchmarked enough to name publicly, but broader mount policy and accepted-outcome templates must stay explicit before it can widen the current served lane

profile remains suppressed pending explicit profile-specific mount, accepted-outcome, and economic policy artifacts; publication note: profile `tassadar.internal_compute.resumable_multi_slice.v1` is benchmarked enough to name publicly, but broader mount policy and accepted-outcome templates must stay explicit before it can widen the current served lane

runtime-support subset is now profile-specific and validator-attachable, but it stays candidate-only until the challenge window closes and the profile-specific authority envelope is satisfied; publication note: profile `tassadar.internal_compute.runtime_support_subset.v1` stays suppressed as a served capability, but now has explicit profile-specific route, mount, and accepted-outcome templates for bounded public use under named policy and portability envelopes

profile remains suppressed pending explicit profile-specific mount, accepted-outcome, and economic policy artifacts; publication note: profile `tassadar.internal_compute.wider_numeric_data_layout.v1` is benchmarked enough to name publicly, but broader mount policy and accepted-outcome templates must stay explicit before it can widen the current served lane

world_mount_dependency_marker: world-mounts remain the owner of canonical profile-specific mount templates outside standalone psionic
kernel_policy_dependency_marker: kernel-policy remains the owner of canonical validator-policy and accepted-outcome-template approval outside standalone psionic
nexus_dependency_marker: nexus remains the owner of canonical candidate-outcome, challenge-window, and accepted-outcome issuance outside standalone psionic
compute_market_dependency_marker: compute-market remains the owner of canonical broad-profile product publication and economic routing outside standalone psionic
claim_boundary: this eval report bridges named broad internal-compute profiles into validator-facing and authority-facing posture without flattening them into one generic broad-compute claim. It keeps accepted-outcome-ready, candidate-only, suppressed, and refused profiles explicit, along with their challenge-window and dependency posture.
summary: Broad general-compute validator bridge now records accepted_outcome_ready_profiles=1, candidate_only_profiles=2, economic_receipt_allowed_profiles=3, suppressed_profiles=4, refused_profiles=1.
